---
title: "Di Liu"
subtitle: "Quantitative scientist · machine learning, forecasting & risk modelling"
toc: false
---

![](images/profile.jpg){.profile-pic width=170}

<!-- Add your photo as images/profile.jpg (or .png — update the extension above). -->

I'm a quantitative scientist and machine learning practitioner with a PhD in
Physics from New York University. I build statistical models, forecasting systems,
and large-scale simulations end-to-end — from data ingestion through model
training and validation to a deployed, monitored product. My work centres on
**uncertainty quantification, time-series and scenario modelling, and turning
complex data into decisions** — in energy systems and, more broadly, in
forecasting and risk. I value work that is reproducible and open, and I care about
communicating technical results clearly to specialist and non-technical audiences
alike.

[GitHub](https://github.com/liudi23) ·
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/di-liu-ph-d-0144aa43/) ·
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Y6LnZG8AAAAJ&hl=en) ·
[Email](mailto:liudisy@gmail.com)

## Selected projects

### 🌡️ Heatwave stress on the GB balancing system
How extreme heat reshapes the imbalance (cash-out) price and the ~18:00 evening
spike — a climate-resilience analysis that ranks the physical drivers rather than
just predicting price. Open data (Elexon BMRS, Sheffield PV_Live, Open-Meteo),
an interpretable decomposition, and a live results app.

[Read the writeup](posts/heatwave-imbalance/index.qmd) ·
[Code](https://github.com/liudi23/heatwave-imbalance)

### ⚡ GB imbalance-price forecasting pipeline
An end-to-end pipeline for short-term GB system-price forecasting — data ingestion,
feature engineering, model training, and monitoring in production, with a Streamlit
dashboard.

[Live app](https://uk-system-price-forecast.streamlit.app/) ·
[Code](https://github.com/liudi23/uk-system-price-forecast)

## Writing

Occasional notes on quantitative modelling and data science — see [Writing](writing.qmd).
